data.title: OTG ranked #31 on the 2025 America's Top 100 Most Loved Workplaces® list for culture, growth, and retention.
data.description: OTG has been ranked as the 31st company in the Top 100 U.S. Companies recognized for culture, employee growth, and retention. The announcement was made in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida on Oct. 17, 2025. Most Loved Workplace® unveiled the 2025 America's Top 100 Most Loved Workplaces®, highlighting companies with exceptional workplace environments. This recognition underscores OTG's commitment to fostering a positive work culture and retaining employees.

data.title: Anna S Rickell recognized as Pinnacle Professional Member of Inner Circle of Excellence for 50 years of work in mental wellness.
data.description: Located in Spartanburg, South Carolina, on October 17, 2025, Anna S. Rickell has been recognized as a Pinnacle Professional Member of the Inner Circle of Excellence for her dedicated efforts in advocating for mental wellness over her fifty-year career in clinical social work. This achievement was highlighted in The Inner Circle publication, acknowledging her significant contributions to the field.

data.title: Ilene Fennoy, MD, MPH recognized as Pinnacle Professional Member Inner Circle of Excellence for Pediatric Endocrinology.
data.description: In a recent publication dated October 17, 2025, Dr. Ilene Fennoy, MD, MPH was recognized as a distinguished member of The Inner Circle for her outstanding work in Pediatric Endocrinology and advocacy for Child Health. Dr. Fennoy has been acknowledged as a Pinnacle Professional Member of the Inner Circle of Excellence. Her notable contributions in the field stand out, showcasing her expertise and dedication to improving pediatric healthcare.

data.title: Mavis expands with 86 new stores since 2024, creating nearly 700 jobs nationwide. Strengthening local communities.
data.description: 86 new Mavis stores opened across the nation since 2024, resulting in the creation of almost 700 jobs. The company, a prominent tire and automotive service provider, is dedicated to supporting local communities by investing in job opportunities and workforce development.

data.title: Stampli Procurement gaining traction, providing total visibility and unified control in procure-to-pay cycle.
data.description: Stampli Procurement is ensuring finance teams are efficiently linking all aspects of procurement, accounts payable (AP), and payments, resulting in complete transparency, quicker processes, and centralized management throughout the procure-to-pay cycle. Based in MOUNTAIN VIEW, Calif., as of Oct. 17, 2025, Stampli Procurement is increasingly becoming the preferred choice for organizations seeking to streamline their financial operations.

data.title: Commercial Credit Group Inc. closes $513,770,000 term ABS for CCGR Trust 2025-2, securing largest transaction to date.
data.description: A commercial equipment finance company based in Charlotte, NC, Commercial Credit Group Inc. (CCG), has successfully finalized its largest asset-backed transaction to date on Oct. 17, 2025. This achievement marks a significant milestone for the company, indicating its continued growth trajectory. The transaction is expected to pave the way for further expansion and development.

data.title: Grant Cardone buys 200 Bitcoin, totaling 500 BTC, in a market dip to store his money securely.
data.description: Cardone Capital, headed by renowned U.S. real estate figure Grant Cardone, recently announced the acquisition of 200 Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) this week. This purchase comes amidst a significant downturn in the crypto market, marking a strategic move by Cardone to expand its holdings. The latest addition of 200 BTC adds to the company's existing 300 BTC, bringing their total Bitcoin ownership to 500. Cardone expressed unwavering confidence in Bitcoin's long-term prospects despite market fluctuations, highlighting his belief in Bitcoin as a more secure asset compared to traditional saving methods. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Global market for Targeted Alpha Therapies valued at $199.6M in 2024, set to grow at 44% CAGR, reaching $1.1B by 2030.
data.description: A study by BCC Research reveals that the global market for Targeted Alpha Therapies (TAT) in cancer treatment was worth $199.6 million in 2024. It is projected to grow at a CAGR of 44% from 2025 to 2030, reaching $1.1 billion. The report discusses market trends, revenue data, cancer type segmentation, challenges, emerging technologies, and ESG initiatives. It also outlines the competitive landscape, including pipeline analysis and company profiles. Factors driving market growth include the rise in cancer cases, demand for precision medicines, unmet treatment needs, strategic initiatives, and new targets for TAT. The report serves as a valuable resource for understanding the current dynamics and outlook of the TAT market.
MGE Energy announces routine dividend payout to shareholders.

data.title: Atlas Energy Solutions Inc. will release Q3 2025 earnings on Nov. 3 after market close, with a conference call on Nov. 4 at 8am CT.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, Atlas Energy Solutions Inc. (NYSE: AESI) shared plans to release its Q3 2025 earnings report after the market closes on Monday, November 3, 2025. A conference call is scheduled for Tuesday, November 4, 2025, at 8:00am Central Time (9:00am Eastern Time) to discuss financial and operational results. A live webcast can be accessed at https://ir.atlas.energy/. Participants are encouraged to join the webcast at least 10 minutes prior to the starting time.

data.title: Gold advocate Peter Schiff warns of impending crypto market crash as Bitcoin hits $106,000, losing 34% of value compared to gold.
data.description: Renowned gold advocate, Peter Schiff, has expressed concerns about Bitcoin's downward trend, forecasting a potential collapse in the broader crypto market. He observed Bitcoin dropping to $106,000 today and highlighted its 34% loss in value compared to gold since its peak in August. Schiff emphasized Bitcoin's significant underperformance against the traditional safe-haven metal. This suggests a possible continuation of the downtrend in the near future. The complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
